docs.title: Git-Map
docs.text:
Version control for ArcGIS web maps.

Git-Map brings familiar Git-style workflows to ArcGIS Online and Portal for ArcGIS. Branch your maps, commit changes, diff versions, merge branches, and push/pull from your portal — all from the command line.

docs.title: Why Git-Map?
docs.text: Problem Git-Map Solution Web maps change without history Every state is a commit you can revisit No way to experiment safely Branches let you iterate without breaking production Collaboration is overwrite-prone Merge branches, review diffs before applying Deployments are manual and fragile gitmap push syncs the right version to Portal Rollbacks require memory gitmap revert restores any previous commit

docs.title: Quick Install
docs.text:
pip install gitmap-core

Then see the Quickstart to run your first commit in under 5 minutes.

docs.title: Core Workflow
docs.text:
gitmap init          # Start tracking a directory
gitmap pull          # Fetch current map from Portal
gitmap commit -m "My change"
gitmap push          # Deploy to Portal

docs.title: Compatibility
docs.text:
• ArcGIS Online — fully supported
  • Portal for ArcGIS (10.8+) — supported
  • Python 3.11 · 3.12 · 3.13
  • OS — macOS, Linux, Windows

Get Started → CLI Reference →

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Git-Map is open-source (MIT). Contributions are welcome!

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/14-TR/Git-Map.git
cd Git-Map
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -e "packages/gitmap_core[dev]"

docs.title: Running Tests
docs.text:
pytest packages/gitmap_core/tests -v

All 663+ tests must pass before opening a PR.

docs.title: Structure
docs.text:
Git-Map/
├── packages/
│   └── gitmap_core/    # Core library (version-controlled here)
├── apps/
│   ├── cli/            # gitmap CLI
│   ├── mcp/            # MCP server
│   └── client/         # GUI client
├── integrations/       # OpenClaw and other integrations
└── docs/               # This documentation site

docs.title: Making Changes
docs.text:
1. Fork the repo and create a feature/ branch.
  2. Write tests for new functionality.
  3. Ensure all tests pass.
  4. Open a PR with a clear description of what changed and why.

docs.title: Docs
docs.text:
This site is built with MkDocs Material. To preview locally:

pip install mkdocs-material
mkdocs serve

Then open http://127.0.0.1:8000.

docs.title: License
docs.text: MIT — see LICENSE.

docs.title: CLI Reference
docs.text: Git-Map provides a Git-inspired CLI for managing web map versions.

docs.title: Command Summary
docs.text: Command Description init Initialize a new repository clone Clone a map from Portal into a new repo status Show working tree status commit Record staged changes as a commit branch List, create, or delete branches checkout Switch branches or restore commits diff Show changes between commits or index log Show commit history merge Merge a branch into the current branch push Push the current branch to Portal pull Pull the latest map from Portal revert Revert to a previous commit stash Stash uncommitted changes temporarily tag Create or list version tags

docs.title: Global Options
docs.text:
gitmap [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

Options:
  --version  Show version and exit.
  --help     Show help and exit.

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
Every command has a --help flag:

gitmap commit --help
gitmap push --help

docs.title: gitmap branch
docs.text: List existing branches, create a new branch, or delete a branch.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: gitmap branch [OPTIONS] [NAME]

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Short Description --delete -d Delete the named branch

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# List all branches
gitmap branch

# Create a new branch
gitmap branch feature/new-layer

# Delete a branch
gitmap branch --delete feature/old-experiment

docs.title: Output (listing)
docs.text:
feature/new-layer
* main
  staging

The * marks the current branch.

docs.title: gitmap checkout
docs.text: Switch to a different branch, or restore the index to a specific commit.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: gitmap checkout [OPTIONS] TARGET

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Switch branches
gitmap checkout main
gitmap checkout feature/new-layer

# Restore index to a specific commit hash
gitmap checkout a3f2c1b0

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Switching branches updates HEAD and loads that branch's latest commit into the index.
  • Checking out a commit hash puts the repo in detached-HEAD mode — create a branch to keep changes.

docs.title: gitmap clone
docs.text: Clone a web map from Portal into a new local repository.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: gitmap clone [OPTIONS] ITEM_ID [PATH]

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Short Description --url -u Portal URL --username Portal username --branch -b Branch name to create (default: main)

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Clone by Portal item ID
gitmap clone abc123def456

# Clone to a named directory
gitmap clone abc123def456 my-project

# Clone from a specific Portal
gitmap clone abc123def456 --url https://portal.example.com

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• clone initializes a new repo, connects to Portal, pulls the specified item, and creates an initial commit — all in one step.
  • Equivalent to gitmap init && gitmap pull && gitmap commit -m "Initial clone".

docs.title: gitmap commit
docs.text: Record changes to the repository by creating a new commit snapshot.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: gitmap commit [OPTIONS]

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Short Required Description --message -m ✓ Commit message --author -a Override the commit author --rationale -r Optional rationale (why this change was made)

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Basic commit
gitmap commit -m "Initial snapshot"

# With author override
gitmap commit -m "Added flood layer" --author "Jane Smith"

# With rationale for audit trail
gitmap commit -m "Removed deprecated layer" -r "Layer decommissioned per ticket #42"

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
Created commit a3f2c1b0

  Message:    Added flood layer
  Author:     Jane Smith
  Timestamp:  2024-06-15T10:23:04
  Layers:     6

Branch 'main' updated to a3f2c1b0

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• If the index matches the last commit (nothing changed), Git-Map prints Nothing to commit and exits cleanly.
  • Commits are immutable snapshots — you can always revert to them.
  • The --rationale field is stored in the commit and shown in gitmap log for audit purposes.

docs.title: gitmap diff
docs.text: Show changes between the current index and the last commit, or between two commits.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: gitmap diff [OPTIONS]

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description --commit Compare a specific commit against its parent

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Diff index vs HEAD
gitmap diff

# Diff a specific commit
gitmap diff --commit a3f2c1b0

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
Diff: index → a3f2c1b0

  + layer "Flood Zones"      (added)
  ~ layer "Parcels"          symbology changed
  - layer "Old Boundaries"   (removed)

docs.title: gitmap init
docs.text: Initialize a new GitMap repository in the current directory (or a specified path).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: gitmap init [OPTIONS] [PATH]

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Short Default Description --project-name -n directory name Human-readable project name --user-name -u "" Default author name for commits --user-email -e "" Default author email

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Initialize in the current directory
gitmap init

# Set project metadata
gitmap init --project-name "Downtown Parcels" --user-name "Jane Smith"

# Initialize at a specific path
gitmap init /path/to/project

docs.title: What It Creates
docs.text:
.gitmap/
├── config.json       # Project settings (name, author, remote)
├── HEAD              # Current branch pointer → refs/heads/main
├── index.json        # Empty staging area
├── refs/
│   └── heads/
│       └── main      # Initial branch
└── objects/
    └── commits/      # Commit storage (empty until first commit)

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Running init in a directory that already has a .gitmap/ folder prints a warning and exits without modifying anything.
  • The default branch is always main.

docs.title: gitmap log
docs.text: Show commit history for the current branch.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: gitmap log [OPTIONS]

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Short Default Description --limit -n 10 Maximum number of commits to show --oneline false Compact one-line format

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
gitmap log
gitmap log -n 5
gitmap log --oneline

docs.title: Output (default)
docs.text:
commit a3f2c1b0
Author:    Jane Smith
Date:      2024-06-15 10:23:04
Message:   Added flood layer

commit 9e1b4a22
Author:    Jane Smith
Date:      2024-06-14 14:05:11
Message:   Initial snapshot

docs.title: Output (--oneline)
docs.text:
a3f2c1b0  Added flood layer
9e1b4a22  Initial snapshot

docs.title: gitmap merge
docs.text: Merge another branch into the current branch.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: gitmap merge [OPTIONS] BRANCH

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
gitmap merge feature/new-layer
gitmap merge staging

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Git-Map performs a logical merge of the map JSON — layer additions, removals, and property changes are combined.
  • Conflicts (e.g., both branches changed the same layer setting differently) are reported for manual resolution.
  • After a successful merge, a merge commit is created automatically.

docs.title: gitmap pull
docs.text: Fetch the latest map data from Portal and update the local staging area (index). Does not auto-commit.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: gitmap pull [OPTIONS]

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Short Description --branch -b Branch to pull (defaults to current branch) --url -u Portal URL --username Portal username --rationale -r Reason for this pull (stored in audit log)

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
gitmap pull
gitmap pull --branch main
gitmap pull --url https://portal.example.com
gitmap pull -r "Syncing production changes after client meeting"

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
Connecting to https://www.arcgis.com...
Authenticated as jsmith
Pulling branch 'main'...

Pulled 'main' from Portal

  Layers: 5

Changes staged. Use 'gitmap diff' to review and 'gitmap commit' to save.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• pull only updates the index. You still need to commit to record the version.
  • Review changes with gitmap diff before committing.

docs.title: gitmap push
docs.text: Push the current branch to ArcGIS Online or Portal as a web map item.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: gitmap push [OPTIONS]

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Short Description --branch -b Branch to push (defaults to current branch) --url -u Portal URL (falls back to config, then PORTAL_URL env var) --username Portal username (or ARCGIS_USERNAME env var) --no-notify Skip sending notifications even if pushing to the production branch --rationale -r Reason for this push (stored in audit log)

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Push current branch
gitmap push

# Push a specific branch
gitmap push --branch staging

# Push to a custom Portal
gitmap push --url https://portal.example.com

# Skip notifications
gitmap push --no-notify

# With rationale
gitmap push -r "Deploying accessibility improvements for sprint 4"

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
Connecting to https://www.arcgis.com...
Authenticated as jsmith
Pushing branch 'main'...

Pushed 'main' to Portal

  Item ID:  abc123def456
  Title:    Downtown Parcels [main]
  URL:      https://www.arcgis.com/home/item.html?id=abc123def456

✓ Notifications sent to 3 user(s)

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Git-Map creates a folder called GitMap in your Portal to organize branch items.
  • If the production branch is configured and --no-notify is not passed, portal group members are notified.
  • Credentials can be stored in config.json via gitmap config to avoid typing them each time.

docs.title: gitmap revert
docs.text: Restore the repository to a previous commit state.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: gitmap revert [OPTIONS] COMMIT

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Revert to a specific commit hash
gitmap revert a3f2c1b0

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• revert loads the target commit's map data into the index and creates a new revert commit — it does not rewrite history.
  • Use gitmap log --oneline to find the commit hash you want to restore.
  • After reverting, use gitmap push to deploy the restored version to Portal.

docs.title: gitmap stash
docs.text: Temporarily set aside uncommitted changes so you can switch context.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: gitmap stash [COMMAND]

docs.title: Subcommands
docs.text: Subcommand Description (none) Stash current changes pop Apply the most recent stash and remove it list Show all stashed states drop Delete a stash entry

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
gitmap stash           # Save changes and clean the index
gitmap stash pop       # Restore the last stash
gitmap stash list      # View saved stashes
gitmap stash drop 0    # Delete stash entry 0

docs.title: gitmap status
docs.text: Show the current state of the working tree — branch, latest commit, and whether there are uncommitted changes.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: gitmap status

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
╭─ GitMap Status ──────────────────────╮
│ On branch: main                      │
╰──────────────────────────────────────╯
Latest commit: a3f2c1b0 - Initial snapshot

Changes not committed:
  + layer "Flood Zones" added
  ~ layer "Parcels" symbology changed

Use "gitmap commit -m <message>" to commit changes

When the working tree is clean:

╭─ GitMap Status ──────────────────╮
│ On branch: main                  │
╰──────────────────────────────────╯
Nothing to commit, working tree clean

docs.title: gitmap tag
docs.text: Create or list version tags.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: gitmap tag [OPTIONS] [NAME]

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description --message / -m Annotate the tag with a message --list List all tags

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Create a lightweight tag
gitmap tag v1.0.0

# Create an annotated tag
gitmap tag v1.0.0 -m "First production release"

# List all tags
gitmap tag --list

docs.title: Core Concepts
docs.text: Git-Map adapts Git's mental model to web maps. If you know Git, you already know most of this.

docs.title: Repository
docs.text:
A repository (.gitmap/ directory) is the version-control database for a project. It stores every commit, branch pointer, and config value. One repository typically tracks one logical map project.

my-maps/
└── .gitmap/
    ├── config.json      # Project settings
    ├── HEAD             # Points to current branch
    ├── index.json       # Staged map data (working area)
    ├── refs/heads/      # Branch pointers
    └── objects/commits/ # Commit snapshots

docs.title: Commit
docs.text:
A commit is a snapshot of the map's JSON at a point in time. Each commit stores:

  • The full operationalLayers and map settings
  • Author, timestamp, and message
  • A reference to the parent commit (forming a chain)

Commits are immutable. You can always return to any commit with gitmap revert or gitmap checkout.

docs.title: Branch
docs.text:
A branch is just a named pointer to a commit. Branches let you maintain parallel versions of a map — for example:

  • main → production map
  • feature/new-symbology → experimental changes
  • staging → QA-ready version

Branches are cheap and fast. Create them freely.

docs.title: Index (Staging Area)
docs.text: The index (index.json) holds the current map data pulled from Portal or manually staged. Running gitmap commit snapshots the index into a new commit.

docs.title: Remote
docs.text: The remote is an ArcGIS Online or Portal organization. gitmap push writes the current branch to Portal as a web map item; gitmap pull reads it back.

docs.title: HEAD
docs.text:
HEAD points to the current branch (or a specific commit in detached-HEAD mode). Most commands operate on HEAD.

Next: CLI Reference →

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.11, 3.12, or 3.13
  • ArcGIS Online account or Portal for ArcGIS 10.8+

docs.title: Install from PyPI
docs.text:
pip install gitmap-core

Verify the install:

gitmap --version

docs.title: Install from Source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/14-TR/Git-Map.git
cd Git-Map
pip install -e "packages/gitmap_core"

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Git-Map reads credentials from environment variables when no config is set:

Variable Description ARCGIS_USERNAME Your ArcGIS/Portal username ARCGIS_PASSWORD Your ArcGIS/Portal password PORTAL_URL Your Portal URL (defaults to https://www.arcgis.com)

You can also store credentials in the repository config file — see Working with Portals.

docs.title: Upgrading
docs.text:
pip install --upgrade gitmap-core

Next: Quickstart →

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text: Get from zero to your first committed map version in under 5 minutes.

docs.title: 1. Initialize a Repository
docs.text:
Navigate to a working directory and initialize Git-Map:

mkdir my-maps
cd my-maps
gitmap init --project-name "My Maps" --user-name "Your Name"

This creates a .gitmap/ directory that tracks all your map versions.

docs.title: 2. Pull a Map from Portal
docs.text:
Authenticate and pull a web map from ArcGIS Online or Portal:

gitmap pull --url https://www.arcgis.com --branch main

Git-Map fetches the map JSON and stages it locally. Nothing is committed yet.

docs.title: 3. Check Status
docs.text:
gitmap status

You'll see something like:

╭─ GitMap Status ─╮
│ On branch: main │
╰─────────────────╯
Changes not committed:
  Staged map with 4 layer(s)
Use "gitmap commit -m <message>" to commit changes

docs.title: 4. Commit
docs.text:
gitmap commit -m "Initial snapshot of production map"

Output:

Created commit a3f2c1b0

  Message:   Initial snapshot of production map
  Author:    Your Name
  Layers:    4

Branch 'main' updated to a3f2c1b0

docs.title: 5. Create a Branch and Experiment
docs.text:
gitmap branch feature/new-basemap
gitmap checkout feature/new-basemap

Make your changes (edit the map in Portal), then pull and commit again:

gitmap pull
gitmap commit -m "Switched to dark basemap"

docs.title: 6. View History
docs.text: gitmap log

docs.title: 7. Diff Two Versions
docs.text: gitmap diff

docs.title: 8. Merge and Push
docs.text:
When the feature is ready, merge it back and push to Portal:

gitmap checkout main
gitmap merge feature/new-basemap
gitmap push

Next: Core Concepts →

docs.title: Branching Strategy
docs.text: Git-Map branches are lightweight — create as many as you need. This guide suggests a strategy that works well for most teams.

docs.title: Recommended Branch Layout
docs.text:
main          ← production (always deployable)
staging       ← QA / client review
feature/*     ← individual changes
hotfix/*      ← urgent production fixes

docs.title: Flow
docs.text:
feature/x  →  staging  →  main
hotfix/y                →  main
  1. Create a feature/ branch for each change.
  2. Merge to staging for review/QA.
  3. Merge staging to main and push to production.
  4. For urgent fixes, branch from main as hotfix/, fix, merge directly to main.

docs.title: Naming Conventions
docs.text: Prefix Use Case feature/ New layers, symbology changes, data updates hotfix/ Urgent production fixes experiment/ Risky changes you might not keep archive/ Preserved state of a historical map version

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• Keep branches short-lived — merge and delete when done.
  • Use gitmap stash to quickly save work-in-progress before switching branches.
  • Tag stable states before major changes: gitmap tag pre-overhaul.

docs.title: Working with Portals
docs.text: Git-Map supports both ArcGIS Online and Portal for ArcGIS (10.8+).

docs.title: Environment Variables (recommended for CI/CD)
docs.text:
export ARCGIS_USERNAME=jsmith
export ARCGIS_PASSWORD=mypassword
export PORTAL_URL=https://myorg.maps.arcgis.com

docs.title: Per-Repo Config
docs.text:
Store the remote URL in the repo config so you don't have to pass --url every time:

gitmap config --remote-url https://myorg.maps.arcgis.com
gitmap config --username jsmith

This writes to .gitmap/config.json. Do not commit credentials to source control.

docs.title: ArcGIS Online
docs.text:
The default Portal URL is https://www.arcgis.com. Named-user authentication is used.

gitmap pull --url https://www.arcgis.com

docs.title: Portal for ArcGIS
docs.text:
Replace with your organization's Portal URL:

gitmap pull --url https://portal.myorg.com/portal

docs.title: Item Organization
docs.text:
When you push, Git-Map creates a GitMap folder in your Portal content and names items using the pattern:

{project-name} [{branch}]

This keeps branches organized and easy to find.

docs.title: Notifications
docs.text:
When pushing to the configured production branch, Git-Map can send Portal notifications to all users in groups that have access to the map. Use --no-notify to suppress this.

gitmap push              # notifies group members
gitmap push --no-notify  # silent push

docs.title: Day-to-Day Workflow
docs.text: This guide walks through the standard Git-Map workflow for managing a production web map.

docs.title: The Golden Rule
docs.text: Never edit the production map directly. Always work on a branch, review the diff, and merge when ready.

docs.title: 1. Start on main, sync first
docs.text:
cd my-map-project
gitmap checkout main
gitmap pull
gitmap commit -m "Sync: latest production state"

docs.title: 2. Create a feature branch
docs.text:
gitmap branch feature/updated-symbology
gitmap checkout feature/updated-symbology

docs.title: 3. Make changes in Portal
docs.text:
Edit the map in ArcGIS Online or Portal as normal. When done:

gitmap pull --branch feature/updated-symbology
gitmap diff           # Review what changed
gitmap commit -m "Updated parcel symbology per client request"

docs.title: 4. Merge and deploy
docs.text:
gitmap checkout main
gitmap merge feature/updated-symbology
gitmap push

docs.title: 5. Tag releases (optional)
docs.text: gitmap tag v2.3 -m "Q2 2024 symbology update"

docs.title: Rollback
docs.text:
If something goes wrong after a push:

gitmap log --oneline        # Find the last good commit
gitmap revert a3f2c1b0      # Restore that state
gitmap push                 # Deploy the fix
